mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate positive from negative returns with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and rising retail sales indicates synchronized consumer and business demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term loses predictive power when added to baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth periods coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in >60% of observations, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 2.5 percent annually while the federal funds rate rises, markets price in restrictive monetary policy and eroding real earnings, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation and rising-rate regime produces positive 20-day returns in >65% of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls indicate a strengthening labor market that supports household income and corporate revenue, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth periods fail to generate positive 60-day SPX returns in >50% of cases, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in >70% of triggered instances, or the threshold signal produces a negative information coefficient over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and rising unemployment rates indicate a broad-based economic contraction, forcing equity markets to discount future earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint deterioration signal yields positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample prediction accuracy relative to single-series models.
